decision: Rework the titlebar project selector to a recursive N-level directory tree with unified styling; add settings-based per-directory enable/disable (cascading); move Settings to a fixed icon-only gear next to the user avatar; replace the default Next favicon with the green daax robot
context: dir-broke.png: repos nested 2+ levels deep (and repo-in-repo like kb) were misclassified as flat non-expandable yellow folders and unreachable; the purple/gray/yellow color scheme was confusing; Settings was a reorderable/hideable nav plugin
rationale: Root cause was one-level-only classification in app/api/workspace/route.ts and one-level tree build in Titlebar.tsx. Fix: recursive bounded walk (MAX_DEPTH=5 + ignore-list, does NOT stop at repos so repo-in-repo stays reachable) emitting a flat list of full relative paths; kept the git|planning|folder type union so shell/ai-coding/code-server/settings consumers are unaffected. New lib/project-tree.ts (unit-tested) builds the nested tree + cascade-disable filter. Titlebar renders a recursive ProjectTreeItem with uniform muted icons (GitBranch=repo, FolderTree=container, Folder=leaf). Added DaaxSettings.disabledProjectDirs and a Project Directory Visibility card in settings/page.tsx. Excluded settings plugin from the admin menu-changer; rendered a fixed gear <Link> before UserMenu. Favicon: app/icon.png + app/favicon.ico regenerated from the branding robot.
validation: typecheck 0 errors, lint 0 errors, prettier clean, 1180 unit tests pass, production build passes; verified over real HTTP: /api/workspace returns 51 dirs with kb exposing 11 nested repos and dx-src/daax-web reachable; /icon.png HTTP 200; Playwright screenshots confirm unified tree, fixed gear, and settings visibility card. Producer: Claude Opus 4.8. Validator: advisor (stronger reviewer, same provider) — cross-provider (Codex) not available in-session.
